heading: “State” defined
label: 1015 “State” defined
text: As used in this chapter, the term “State” includes the several States, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, Guam, and the District of Columbia.
notes: Amendments1956—Act , included “Guam” in definition of State.Aug. 1, 1956

Admission of Alaska and Hawaii to StatehoodAlaska was admitted into the Union on , on issuance of Proc. No. 3269, , 24 F.R. 81, 73 Stat. c16, and Hawaii was admitted into the Union on , on issuance of Proc. No. 3309, , 24 F.R. 6868, 73 Stat. c74. For Alaska Statehood Law, see , , , set out as a note preceding , Territories and Insular Possessions.
